name: Star Arctic Hotel
amenities: spa with sauna and hot tub

content.verdict: Star Arctic Hotel is the intimate, elegant counterpoint to Saariselkä's larger Kakslauttanen igloo village — only 38 suites perched on top of Kaunispää fell, every one of them with floor-to-ceiling glass and a private fell-view balcony. Where Kakslauttanen scatters small igloos across the forest, Star Arctic concentrates the experience in a single architecturally striking building, with three restaurants, a proper spa, and an outdoor jacuzzi that looks out across rolling fells to a horizon almost untouched by light. For honeymooners who want glass-walled aurora viewing, but in a real hotel rather than a freestanding pod, this is the obvious choice in the Saariselkä area. The fell-top setting is the star — sunrise paints the surrounding fells pink, the elevation makes for huge open horizons in every direction, and the high ceiling height of the suites gives the rooms an airy, design-led feel that you do not get in a small igloo. Ski lifts run from below the hotel into Saariselkä's quiet ski area; husky kennels and reindeer farms cluster nearby; Urho Kekkonen National Park starts at the back door.
content.best_room: Premium Aurora Suite with private balcony and outdoor hot tub — a glass-fronted top-floor suite with a king bed facing the fell view, a soaking tub inside, and a private balcony with a hot tub for aurora watching outside.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival via Ivalo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Ivalo (IVL), 30-minute transfer to Saariselkä, and settle into your Premium Aurora Suite. Dinner at one of the hotel's three restaurants and a first aurora watch from the balcony hot tub.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Saariselkä skiing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Ski-in / ski-out morning on the quiet, well-groomed slopes below the hotel, lunch at the fell-top cafe, and afternoon spa session.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Husky safari
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day husky sled experience with a partner kennel near Saariselkä, lunch in a kota, and an evening aurora photography session on the fell-top.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Reindeer farm and Sami stories
itinerary_7_nights.description: Visit a working reindeer farm, sleigh ride, and a traditional Sami-flavoured dinner in a kota tepee. Long sauna and balcony aurora watch back at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snowmobile expedition
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day guided snowmobile expedition into Urho Kekkonen National Park, ice-fishing stop on a frozen lake, and a fireside lunch in a wilderness cabin.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snowshoe and ice swim
itinerary_7_nights.description: Snowshoe through old-growth forest, optional ice-swimming session at a partner sauna, and a tasting-menu dinner at the hotel.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final fell breakfast
itinerary_7_nights.description: Sunrise from the balcony, a last fell-top walk, breakfast at the hotel, and a short transfer back to Ivalo for your flight home.

caveats: Aurora viewing depends entirely on clear skies — the fell-top position is excellent, but cloud cannot be planned around, so book a longer stay for safety.
Winter temperatures on Kaunispää fell are exposed and frequently between -15°C and -25°C with significant wind chill — bring or rent serious gear.
Star Arctic is a premium-priced boutique and Premium Aurora Suites with hot tubs sell out 6-12 months ahead in peak winter.
December days are extremely short (sun up around 11am, down around 1pm) — plan most outdoor activities tightly within daylight.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the Premium Aurora Suites with private balcony and hot tub are designed for couples, with glass walls facing the fells for aurora viewing from the bed.

faqs.question: Will we see the northern lights?
faqs.answer: Saariselkä and Kaunispää fell sit in the high-aurora zone with minimal light pollution, but aurora is weather-dependent — book 4-6 nights for a strong chance.

faqs.question: What is the best month for aurora?
faqs.answer: Late September through March, with February and March typically the most reliable months for clear, dark skies.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: January through March for snow and aurora, December for the deep midwinter atmosphere, and August/September for the autumn ruska colour change on the fells.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: A Premium Aurora Suite with private hot tub, husky and reindeer experiences, snowmobile day, and a tasting-menu dinner in the hotel restaurant.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Ivalo (IVL) via Helsinki, take a 30-minute transfer to Saariselkä, and the hotel can arrange the journey directly.
